title: Minecraft, But Every 30 Seconds You Swap Places With a Mob
description: A Fun Datapack that randomly swaps your coordinates with any loaded mod(hostile/passive).
body:
# Minecraft, But Every 30 Seconds You Swap Places With A Random Mob

A fun survival datapack that randomly swaps your coordinates with a loaded entity.

## Overview

As the name suggests, you randomly swap places with a random mob or player. By default the interval is set to 30 seconds, which you can change using the trigger command below.

There isn't a particular radius or anything — you will swap places with any loaded entity in the world. The idea is inspired by the movie Freaky Friday (though they swap bodies, not places. I might make something like that in the future).

## Core Mechanics

- **Swap System**: Independent timers per player
- **Timing**: 30 second intervals (default)
- **Valid Targets**: Hostile mobs, passive mobs, and other players

## Features

- ✓ Customizable timer via `/trigger ff_set_timer set <seconds>`
- ✓ Filters out dropped items, projectiles, and non-entity objects
- ✓ Multiplayer compatible
- ✓ Works in existing worlds
- ✓ No resource pack dependencies
- ✓ Lightweight — no performance impact

## Installation

1. Download the datapack
2. Place it in your world's `datapacks` folder
3. Run `/reload` in-game
4. Done — the timer starts immediately

## Technical Requirements

- **Minecraft Version**: 1.21+
- **Vanilla Datapack**: Yes
- **Singleplayer**: Yes
- **Multiplayer**: Yes
- **Performance**: Extremely lightweight

## FAQ

**Can I control how often we swap?**
Yes, use `/trigger ff_set_timer set <seconds>` to change the interval at any time.

**Does it cause server lag?**
No.

**Will it swap me with dropped items or arrows?**
No, filtering prevents that.

**Compatible with other datapacks?**
Yes.

**Works in existing worlds?**
Yes.
